paper_title: Wavelet Analysis of X-ray Density Function of Tree Ring Structure

methods.method_name: Wavelet Analysis of X-ray Density Profiles
methods.description: This method involves using wavelet transformation to analyze the X-ray density profiles of tree rings. The wavelet transform is used to identify physical changes and subtle variations in the density of annual rings, providing insights into growth cycle periods and environmental anomalies.

steps.description: The continuous wavelet transform of a discrete sequence x n (density function of a tree) is defined as the convolution of x n with a scaled and translated version of the mother wavelet ψ 0 (η).
steps.searchable_summary: perform continuous wavelet transform on tree density function

steps.description: In the wavelet analysis of the X-ray density profiles of the three sugi trees, the probability amplitudes vary as a function of frequency and distance.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze probability amplitudes in wavelet transformed data

steps.description: The calculated wavelet plots presented in Figure 1 a, b and c show different growth cycle periods of the tree.
steps.searchable_summary: generate wavelet plots to visualize growth cycle periods

steps.description: Morlet mother-wavelet basis set was used in the case pf28 and nf95 samples, while the Paul (c) basis set was used for the nf221 sample.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Morlet and Paul mother-wavelet basis sets to samples
outputs: wavelet transformed data with specific basis sets
